## [00:00 - 03:00]

Alright, 10Xer, let's get into these checklists. This first checklist is what you're going to use every time you create a new sales funnel. This will give you ideas, but also confidence that you're doing it right the first time. The first phase, and I'm going to zoom in here a little bit, at the very beginning of this process, you always want to plan it before you build it. So they're going to land on this page, then after this page, they go to this page to buy a product, then they go to an upsell, just sketch it all out before you build it. And then last but not least, in addition to sketching out the pages that you want to build, you also want to map out what follow-up emails do you want to include. So for example, if somebody opts in, what are they going to get from there, or if they buy a product, what emails do they get if they abandon a cart, whatever it might be, just always as a golden rule, try to plan it. Doesn't need to be a long plan, but at least have an idea of what you want to build before you build it. Okay? So the next step, we're going to actually get into the building and start setting the sucker up. ## [03:00 - 06:01]

You're going to set up those emails that you've already planned out. If you've got opt-in forms, you need to obviously set those things up. Calendar widgets. But know this, that the headline is the most important part of the page because it's what's going to grab people's attention or it's not. So you really got to nail that headline. Any kind of elements, individual things that you want to add, maybe those video or you want to add audio or just some text boxes or some images, add whatever kind of stuff that you want to your page, then you're going to customize your font and your design, make it look and how you want. Okay, you'll see that in the training, but just make sure that you never put a funnel live without looking at it both on desktop and on mobile. Okay, then you're going to test every step of your funnel. Make sure that every single step that a user could possibly go to not only works on desktop, but also on mobile. ## [06:01 - 09:02]

How do you make this thing better? So this is really an idea sheet to help you continue to make this thing better every single day. Let's zoom on in here. So I want to start with big funnel ideas. So the overall objectives. Here's what I mean by this. A lot of times, you might be trying to, for example, sell a high ticket item. Let's just say you sell a product for $1,000. There's a lot of different ways that you can sell that product for $1,000. Maybe you have a squeeze page that collects information, then it goes to a low ticket item and then it has an upsell for $1,000. Or maybe you actually send them to a webinar and then you sell them on the webinar. So the most important thing is whenever you're thinking about testing or optimizing, you always start with the big picture things first. So think about the overall objectives and how can you go about doing it differently and test those things first. Next big idea is the offer and or the pricing. So that product might be $1,000, but what if we sold it for $297? I'll tell you that I had one of the biggest business breakthroughs in my life when we changed the price of our product from $997 to $297 and we sold five times as many and just absolutely crushed it. So think about the offer and the pricing. Next is there's some sort of bump, some sort of check mark upsell or some sort of one click upsell that we can add that can really move the needle. Functionality of the pages, here's what I mean by functionality is when somebody lands on the page, before you worry about what colors the button and these small little details, really think about what's the functionality. I'll give you a really good example here. Years ago we used to always have our opt-in forms directly on the page. Well then somebody came up with a clever idea of well what happens if we put a button here and when that button is clicked, it opens up the opt-in form, which you might think makes it harder, but we saw a major bump. Now it's funny how nowadays it's actually going back the other way. So things are always changing, but when you're thinking about what do I test and what are the things that I need to see if I can try out in order to really move the needle on my page, think about the functionality of the pages first and then also the order of the pages. Sometimes having one upsell before another can really get a lot different yield in terms of average cart value. So those are really the big funnel ideas to be thinking about. Now in regards to optimization, the first thing that I mentioned earlier is the hook, the hook or the headline. What's that top language and messaging that's really drawing people in? Next thing I recommend is utilizing video or optimizing video and I'll just tell you this, don't think that using video is better every time because it's not. In fact, what we found is one of the things that kills opt-in rate is having a video on

## [09:02 - 12:06]

the opt-in page. Now is that every time? Not really. When it's Grant Cardone, that's obviously a major advantage, but for you if you're thinking, oh man, I have to have a video on my opt-in page, you don't have to. So that's something that can move the needle in either direction. Then the overall design of the page. So for example, rather than thinking about the small little headline color, think about, for example, we ran a split test recently that just put a dark page versus a light page and the light page did 25% better than the dark page. So think about the overall design theme. Next is adding or removing elements. So sometimes it's good to have more, sometimes it's good to have less. So what can you add or what can you take away from the page to make it better? Next is copy and colors on the page. So we're talking about the specific little areas of, okay, now we're describing this product, how do we describe that product? Or what are the individual colors of can we make this button orange or can we make it red? Okay, what I'm really trying to drive at here is that people focus on those things first when really those are some of the last things that you should be focusing on optimizing. So coming up here, we're going to talk about potential additions. These are things that you can add to your funnel to really bump up your conversion rate. So first of all, adding email or text follow up as a general rule, the more emails you add, the more text you add, the higher conversion rate you're going to get popups. We already mentioned there's various kinds. You can have popups that open upon click of a button. You can also have popups that are there that are referred to as exit intent popups. So if somebody tries to leave your page, they open, you can add a countdown timer. One of the greatest conversion improvements on a page is a countdown timer. It's very easy to do. It can really move the needle. Adding a live chat support widget. You may or may not have the capability to do this, but that's always a good thing if you can. And then social proof widgets. And we talk more about this in the manual, but these are the things that say, Bob just bought this. All right, so those are things that you can potentially add on. Okay, the last thing that I want to mention here is the overall strategy. So we talked a lot about the things that you optimize, but let's talk about the strategy in which you do that. So first one is AB split testing. This is probably the most tried and true bread and butter way. All you do is you make two pages. And if you're using a system like ClickFunnels, it's very easy. So for example, you could test any of these elements that you wanted. So for example, okay, cool, let's test a page with video and let's test a page without video. Then we just run half the traffic to one and half the traffic to other and we see which one works. Okay, so always be running split tests on your most important pages. Don't think you have to do it on all pages, but the ones that are most important are the ones that the most people see and make you the most money. Okay, customizing for specific audiences. If you're trying to figure out a way, how do I get a bump in my conversion?

## [12:06 - 13:23]

Consider the possibility of maybe you can niche down your landing page, for example. So let's just say you had a landing page that was addressing a male audience and a landing page that was addressing a female audience. Or you've got a certain subsection of your client. Just think about the possibility of rather than just having these global improvements, you're actually talking to a specific audience. And then last but not least is making improvements to your automated follow up, which is the adding the text and the emails and those kinds of things. And I put a note down here that says optimization is never done. Keep going for what it's worth. We developed funnels that have ran for over a decade and are still being optimized to this day. So what I do want to say is obviously you got a lot of good ideas in here, but what I don't want to do is overwhelm you. So just remember this, you don't need to do all of these things. And to be frank, if you just get a funnel up and running, you're already winning and you can take this thing one step at a time. So just remember that all of these things are here for you to help give you ideas to get better and to improve. But the most important thing is you don't get overwhelmed. You get in the game and you get this thing rocking.
